seoTitle: How to Export a Trello Board to PDF
metaDescription: Turn your Trello board into a clean PDF using print layout settings or a reporting Power-Up, complete with labels and card details.
intro: Creating an offline archive or client-ready summary of your Trello board takes a bit of layout tweaking to avoid messy formatting. This guide covers preparing your board for export and using either the native print options or a dedicated reporting Power-Up.

steps.heading: Find the print and export settings
steps.body: Open the board options hierarchy to locate the centralized print and export gateway.

steps.heading: Choose landscape orientation
steps.body: Adjust your layout parameters, selecting landscape orientation and minimal page margins for a wider, cleaner fit.

steps.heading: Enable background graphics
steps.body: Turn on background graphics so your color labels and card covers display properly in the printed output.

steps.heading: Preview before exporting
steps.body: Check your print preview to confirm the layout looks correct before finalizing the PDF.

steps.heading: Try a reporting Power-Up for more control
steps.body: Use a tool like Board Export by Blue Cat to transform your raw tasks into a more structured, linear reporting PDF.

steps.heading: Filter your cards first
steps.body: Clean up your export by filtering out irrelevant cards or variables to keep the final document from feeling cluttered.

faq.question: Can I export just part of my board instead of the whole thing?
faq.answer: Yes, filtering your cards before exporting helps you produce a cleaner, more focused PDF.

faq.question: Why don't my color labels show up in the printed PDF?
faq.answer: You likely need to enable the background graphics toggle in your print settings so colors render correctly.

faq.question: Is there a way to get a more polished report format?
faq.answer: Yes, a Power-Up like Board Export by Blue Cat can generate a more structured, corporate-style report compared to a basic print export.

faq.question: What orientation works best for exporting a board?
faq.answer: Landscape orientation with minimal margins typically fits a wide Kanban layout better than portrait mode.
